title: Podostemum stylosum
taxonomy: Podostemum stylosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Podostemaceae. This family is a group of aquatic plants commonly known as riverweed or mudseed. Podostemum stylosum is a member of the genus Podostemum, which contains a total of 14 species. The plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Central and South America. As a member of the Podostemaceae family, Podostemum stylosum is closely related to other aquatic plant species found in similar environments.

intro: Podostemum stylosum, also known as the water caltrop or stylosum, is a rare aquatic plant species belonging to the family Podostemaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a useful remedy for treating various skin conditions and infections. The bioactive constituents of P. stylosum include podophyllotoxin, podorhizin, and stylosumine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, P. stylosum is often used to prepare herbal extracts, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into creams and ointments for topical application.
